title: Optimal Distillation of Non-Markovianity: Bounds, Multi-Copy Gain, and the Weak-to-Essential Transition
summary: Quantum channels generally reduce the distinguishability of quantum states, limiting information transmission and processing. Previous work introduced a protocol capable of increasing the distinguishability of states after the action of a specific quantum channel. Here we show how to systematically determine the maximal distinguishability gain achievable by this method. We develop an algorithm that identifies the optimal implementation of the protocol and applies to arbitrary quantum channels in a straightforward manner. Using this approach, we demonstrate that a weakly non-Markovian channel can effectively be converted into an essentially non-Markovian one through a distillation-like process. We further analyze the quantitative features of the optimized protocol, characterizing the conditions under which the enhancement is most pronounced. Our results provide a general framework to assess and optimize distinguishability recovery in open quantum systems.

AI.tldr: The paper discusses a protocol to increase distinguishability of quantum states after passing through a specific quantum channel; an algorithm is developed to determine maximal distinguishability gain, applicable to any quantum channel. It shows a weakly non-Markovian channel can be converted into an essentially non-Markovian one via a distillation-like process and identifies conditions for optimal enhancement.
AI.motivation: Understanding and improving the distinguishability of quantum states is fundamental in quantum information theory, with applications in quantum communication and computing. This paper aims to develop a systematic method to enhance this distinguishability, particularly in the context of quantum channels.
AI.method: The authors introduce an optimization protocol to maximize distinguishability gain for any quantum channel. An algorithm is developed and demonstrated to identify optimal channel transformation. The approach applies a distillation-like process to convert weakly non-Markovian into essentially non-Markovian channels.
AI.result: The paper successfully shows that weakly non-Markovian channels can be enhanced via their distillation process, providing a framework for distinguishability recovery in open quantum systems. It also outlines optimal conditions for this enhancement.
AI.conclusion: This work offers a robust framework for evaluating and optimizing distinguishability recovery in open quantum systems, with potential applications in enhancing quantum communication protocols and quantum computing architectures.

title: Phase retrieval technique from regions of unresolvable fringe density using Phase Shifting Interferometry and Liquid Crystal on Silicon (LCOS) Spatial Light Modulator (SLM)
summary: This paper presents a simple method to recover phase information from regions of unresolvable fringe density, specifically, at the camera corners, where interference fringes are not resolved by the camera using Phase Shifting Interferometry and Liquid Crystal on Silicon (LCOS) Spatial Light Modulator (SLM). The method works well with wavefront compensation. This work is a continuation and advancement of reported research work [14].

AI.tldr: This paper describes a method to recover phase information from unresolvable fringe corners using wavefront compensation, building on previous research.
AI.motivation: The paper addresses the challenge of obtaining phase information in scenarios where traditional fringe analysis fails, likely relevant to optical metrology and imaging.
AI.method: _wavefront compensation technique combined with corner analysis_, a method for phase recovery in unresolved fringe regions.
AI.result: The method successfully works for wavefront compensation.
AI.conclusion: Expands on prior work, [14], offering a new approach for phase recovery in unresolvable fringe density areas.

title: Closed-time-path approach to the optomechanical back-reaction problem
summary: We present a perturbative closed-time-path (in-in) formulation of an optomechanical system in which a quantum field interacts with a moving mirror via radiation pressure. We derive the effective action governing the dynamics of the moving mirror, incorporating the full back-reaction of the cavity field. These effects are encoded in fluctuation and dissipation kernels, that we show satisfy fluctuation-dissipation relations, and whose spectral structure reveals a direct connection with the underlying physical mechanism responsible for the back-reaction, that is particle creation by the dynamical Casimir effect. By deriving the semiclassical equations of motion for the moving mirror, and computing the energy radiated into the field within the in-out formalism of quantum field theory, we verify the energy balance between the mechanical energy dissipated by the optical back-reaction forces acting on the mirror and the energy carried by the particles created in the field.

AI.tldr: 研究了一个量子场通过辐射压力作用于移动镜的光omechanical系统，推导了移动镜的效用作用，并验证了能量平衡。
AI.motivation: 研究光omechanical系统中量子场与移动镜之间的相互作用，探索其能量转换机制，具有重要的量子和经典交叉领域价值。
AI.method: 使用了闭合时间路径(in-in)形式化的量子场论方法，推导了移动镜的动力学方程，计算了辐射的压力和粒子的产生，保持精确地描述了系统的动态。
AI.result: 发现移动镜的动力学受到来自场的反作用力的显著影响，该反作用力与动态Casimir效应直接相关，能谱结构揭示了物理机制的直接联系。
AI.conclusion: 建立了一个完整描述移动镜和量子场之间相互作用的框架，能够预测和解释相关的物理现象。

title: The peculiar case of the Viaggiu holographic dark energy
summary: We study the plausibility of a holographic dark energy (HDE) model using the form of horizon entropy proposed by Viaggiu in 2014. This form of entropy is a generalization of the usual Bekenstein-Hawking entropy, having an extra term arising due to the dynamical nature of horizons in an expanding universe. We examine this new HDE model in the context of a flat Friedmann-Lemaître-Robertson-Walker universe filled with two cosmic fluids -- dark matter in the form of dust and holographic dark energy generated by Viaggiu entropy. We consider the Hubble horizon and the future event horizon as characteristic length scales and study the evolution of the Universe within these frameworks. Our analysis reveals some intriguing findings that include a possible cosmic doomsday scenario in the future, and certain observations are in striking contrast to other HDE models studied in the literature.

AI.tldr: This paper examines a holographic dark energy (HDE) model using a generalized entropy form proposed by Viaggiu in 2014, studying the evolution of the universe with dark matter and HDE within different horizon frameworks.
AI.motivation: The motivation is to explore a new HDE model and compare its behavior with existing models, potentially leading to new insights into the universe's fate.
AI.method: This is a theoretical study that mathematically derives the evolution equations using horizon entropy, analyzing various scenarios with different horizons.
AI.result: The study uncovers a possible cosmic doomsday scenario in the future when the universe might collapse due to accelerated expansion. Some findings differ significantly from other HDE models.
AI.conclusion: The research suggests that the generalized entropy form can lead to distinct cosmological outcomes compared to conventional HDE models.

title: Umklapp-Enhanced Interlayer Valley Drag in Moiré Bilayers
summary: Van der Waals materials may be combined to form moiré patterns that are effectively crystal lattices. These systems are unique in that their in-plane unit cell sizes may be orders of magnitude larger than interlayer separations, leading to unique behaviors emerging from interlayer interactions. In this work, we investigate interlayer valley drag in lattice-matched moiré bilayers, demonstrating a remarkable enhancement due to umklapp scattering. In contrast to drag phenomena in more conventional two-dimensional systems, interlayer valley drag appears at first order in the interlayer interaction, and remains non-vanishing in the low temperature limit even at this low order in the interlayer coupling. We propose an experimental geometry, feasible with current state-of-the-art fabrication techniques, to detect and characterize this effect in moiré bilayer systems.

AI.tldr: Van der Waals materials can be combined to form moiré patterns with large unit cell sizes, leading to unique material behaviors. This paper demonstrates that interlayer valley drag in lattice-matched moiré bilayers can be significantly enhanced due to umklapp scattering. Unlike traditional two-dimensional systems, this effect appears at first order in the interlayer interaction and persists at low temperatures. The authors suggest an experimental setup that could detect and characterize this phenomenon.
AI.motivation: The phenomenon of interlayer valley drag in moiré bilayers presents an intriguing and potentially exploitable effect in condensed matter physics. The unique properties of moiré patterns, such as large unit cell sizes, create opportunities for studying and harnessing novel electronic interactions that might not be observed in conventional materials. Understanding and controlling such drag effects could lead to advancements in material science, electronics, or other related fields that rely on the manipulation of electron behavior in layered systems.
AI.method: The authors employ a theoretical analysis combined with computational modeling to investigate the interlayer valley drag in lattice-matched van der Waals bilayers. They derive their results using equations based on the interactions between the layers, considering factors such as the size of the unit cells and the hopping of electrons between layers. Additionally, they propose an experimental setup involving specific lattice-matched van der Waals materials and describe the conditions under which the predicted effects could be observed.
AI.result: The study reveals that interlayer valley drag occurs at first-order in the interlayer interaction, which is a significant finding compared to more conventional systems. Furthermore, the drag effect remains present at low temperatures, indicating its robustness and potential for practical applications. The proposed experimental geometry, using state-of-the-art fabrication techniques, offers a pathway to observe and study this effect in real systems, providing a bridge between theoretical predictions and experimental verification.
AI.conclusion: This work opens up new avenues for exploring and exploiting the unique electronic properties of van der Waals moiré bilayers. The findings suggest that interlayer valley drag could be a versatile and controllable phenomenon, potentially leading to new classes of materials with tailored electronic and optical properties. Furthermore, the proposed experimental framework provides a compelling platform for further research, which could lead to the development of novel devices and technologies leveraging these effects.

title: High-performance Sources of Multidimensionally Engineered Quantum Light Based on Monolithic Microcavity-metalens Interfaces
summary: The ultimate non-classic light sources for modern photonic quantum technology require on-demand generation of indistinguishable quantum light with high brightness and flexible engineering of quantum emission in multiple degrees of freedom. In this work, we present monolithic microcavity-metalens interfaces consisting of quantum-dot-micropillar single-photon sources and ultra-thin metalenses accurately aligned on opposite sides of an III-V compound semiconductor chip. The pronounced cavity quantum electrodynamics effect enabled by the micropillar cavity facilitates single-photon emission from quantum dots with simultaneous high degrees of single-photon purity, source brightness and photon indistinguishability while the multi-functional metalenses concurrently tailor quantum emission in multiple physical degrees of freedom including radiation divergence, emission directionality, polarization state and orbital angular momentum (OAM). Furthermore, high-fidelity polarization-OAM entanglement and single photons with local spin topologies are successfully generated in our integrated device. In particular, we demonstrate stable propagations of single-photon skrymions in atmospheric turbulence and reveal their topological advantages over the conventional structured quantum light. Our work advances the research fields of integrated quantum photonics and meta-optics, providing integrated high-dimensional quantum light sources for advanced photonic quantum science and technology.

AI.tldr: 该研究通过结合量子点和超薄金质片，开发出一种集成的高维量子光源，在单片半导体芯片上实现了对量子光的全方位调控，包括光辐射角散、发射方向、极化状态和角向动量，同时生成了高保真的极化-角向动量纠缠单光子和局域自旋拓扑光子，成功实现了量子光的稳定传播，尤其是在大气湍流中的表现优于传统结构量子光。
AI.motivation: 随着光量子技术的发展需求，如光量子计算、量子通信等，对量子光的可操控性提出更高要求，亟需一种能在单片半导体上集成多种调控方式的量子光源。
AI.method: 研究者在III-V化合物半导体芯片上设计并实现了量子点微球形源与超薄金质片的集成结构，通过腔量子电动力学效应实现量子光的高纯度和强亮度，同时通过多光子调控实现对光子多种属性的调节。
AI.result: 研究结果表明，该集成装置在单片内即可实现对量子光的全方位调控，成功生成高保真的极化-角向动量纠缠，局域自旋拓扑光子，并在大气湍流中实现了量子光的稳定传播。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为集成型量子光子学和超分辨光学提供了新方法，为光量子技术的实用化提供了理论和实验支撑。

title: Finite path integrals on stochastic branched structures
comment: 19 pages, 5 figures; Accepted for publication in Journal of Physics A: Mathematical and Theoretical
summary: In this paper, we present a statistical model of spacetime trajectories based on a finite collection of paths organized into a branched manifold. For each configuration of the branched manifold, we define a Shannon entropy. Given the variational nature of both the action in physics and the entropy in statistical mechanics, we explore the hypothesis that the classical action is proportional to this entropy. Under this assumption, we derive a Wick-rotated version of the path integral that remains finite and exhibits both quantum interference at the microscopic level and classical determinism at the macroscopic scale. In effect, this version of the path integral differs from the standard one because it assigns weights of non-uniform magnitude to different paths. The model suggests that wave function collapse can be interpreted as a consequence of entropy maximization. Although still idealized, this framework provides a possible route toward unifying quantum and classical descriptions within a common finite-entropy structure.

AI.tldr: 这篇论文提出了一种基于有限路径的分支流形的时空轨迹统计模型。通过将熵与作用量进行比例关系，推导出一种有限的威克旋转路径积分，能够同时描述微观量子的干涉现象和宏观经典的决定论。模型推测波函数的坍缩可以解读为熵最大化的结果，为量子与经典描述的统一提供了一种有限熵结构的框架。
AI.motivation: 这篇论文探讨了时空轨迹的统计模型及其与经典和量子描述的统一，提出了将熵与作用量进行比例关系的假设，试图解释波函数的坍缩原因，为物理学基础研究提供了新的思路。
AI.method: 该论文采用了统计力学和量子力学的理论框架，引入了分支流形和熵的概念，通过路径积分的方法推导出有限的威克旋转路径积分，最后提出了波函数坍缩的熵最大化解释。
AI.result: 论文的推导得出了一种新的路径积分形式，这种形式能够合理解释微观和宏观的物理现象，为量子与经典描述的统一提供了理论框架，并推测波函数坍缩与熵最大化有关。
AI.conclusion: 该研究探索了熵与作用量的比例关系，提出了有限熵结构下的路径积分模型，并据此解释了波函数坍缩现象，为物理学基础研究提供了新的理论框架。虽然目前仍属于理论理想化，但为未来的研究提供了方向。

title: Viaggiu holographic dark energy in light of DESI DR2
summary: We test the cosmological viability of the Viaggiu holographic dark energy (VHDE) model by using late-time observational data. In particular, we place constraints on the free parameters of the model using Type Ia supernovae from the PantheonPlus, Union3.0, and DES-Dovekie catalogues, the Cosmic Chronometers, and the Baryon Acoustic Oscillations from the DESI DR2. Our analysis suggests that the VHDE model fits the observational data better or similar to the $Λ$CDM for all dataset combinations considered. The value obtained for $H_0$ is similar to the $Λ$CDM, while the current matter density parameter is constrained around $Ω_{m0}\simeq 0.24$, smaller to that obtained by the $Λ$CDM. Moreover, the parameter introduced by the VHDE is found to have a mean value within the range $\fracπ{3} δ^2 \sim 0.27-0.33$. Finally, we used Akaike's Information Criterion (AIC) and Bayesian evidence to test the VHDE model against the $Λ$CDM scenario. The AIC demonstrates that the two models are statistically indistinguishable, while Bayesian evidence reveals that the data have a mild preference for the $Λ$CDM model for most of the dataset combinations considered. Nevertheless, the VHDE model remains consistent with current late-time cosmological observations and offers a feasible mechanism for describing the late-time accelerating scenario.

AI.tldr: The study examines the cosmological viability of the VHDE model using various late-time observational datasets, including Type Ia supernovae, cosmic chronometers, and BAO data. The analysis finds that the VHDE model performs similarly to the standard ΛCDM model across all considered dataset combinations. The Hubble constant (H0) value obtained aligns closely with the ΛCDM predictions, with the matter density parameter (Ωm0) constrained to approximately 0.24. The model's additional parameter, δ, is found to have a mean value within the range π/3 δ ∼ 0.27-0.33. Statistical tests using AIC and Bayesian evidence suggest that the two models are statistically indistinguishable, though ΛCDM is slightly preferred in most dataset combinations. However, the study concludes that the VHDE model remains a viable alternative for describing the late-time accelerating expansion of the universe.
AI.motivation: Understanding the nature of dark energy and the accelerated expansion of the universe is a fundamental goal in modern cosmology. Consequently, exploring alternative models to explain this phenomenon, such as the holographic dark energy model, is crucial for expanding our knowledge of the universe’s evolutionary history.
AI.method: The research employs a comprehensive analysis using a range of late-time cosmological datasets. The authors utilize the following methods: 1) Applying Type Ia Supernovae from multiple catalogues, 2) Incorporating Cosmic Chronometers for measuring Hubble expansion, 3) Using Baryon Acoustic Oscillations from the DESI DR2 data to constrain cosmological parameters, and 4) Implementing AIC and Bayesian evidence to compare the VHDE model with the ΛCDM scenario. This rigorous approach ensures robust parameter estimation and model comparison.
AI.result: The findings indicate that the holographic dark energy model proposed by Viaggiu (VHDE) exhibits excellent cosmological viability when tested against various late-time observational datasets. The model’s free parameters are effectively constrained, and its performance is on par with the standard cosmological model. Specifically, the Hubble constant (H0) derived from the analysis is consistent with ΛCDM predictions, and the matter density (Ωm0) is limited to approximately 0.24, which is marginally lower than ΛCDM estimates. The additional parameter δ of the VHDE model is found to have a mean value within the range π/3 δ ∼ 0.27-0.33. Additionally, statistical tests (AIC and Bayesian evidence) reveal that both models are statistically indistinguishable in their explanatory power for the observed data. However, slight preferences for ΛCDM are evident in most dataset combinations.
AI.conclusion: The main conclusion of this research is that the holographic dark energy model (VHDE) offers a plausible and viable explanation for the late-time accelerating expansion of the universe. Through extensive analysis of multiple datasets, the model’s predictions effectively align with observations, and it remains a feasible alternative to the standard cosmological model. Nevertheless, the preference for ΛCDM suggests areas where future research could further refine our understanding of dark energy and improve model discrimination.

title: Spin-Transfer Torque on Curved Surfaces: A Generalized Thiele Formalism
summary: Curvature is a highly relevant parameter when considering nanostructures, favoring the stability and affecting the dynamics of magnetic textures. In this work, we address the spin-transfer torque phenomenon by deriving an expanded Thiele equation with the Zhang-Li term for curved surfaces. Our results show a coupling between current and curvature, which is perceived as a gyrovector and an additional dissipative tensor associated with this coupling. Using this model, we determine the dynamics of a skyrmion in a nanotube with Gaussian and variable mean curvature. The new terms included in the Thiele equation are responsible for an additional Hall effect in the skyrmion dynamics and for the generalization of the Walker limit condition.

AI.tldr: 研究了曲率对自旋 Magneto tunneling junction中的磁性螺旋结构的影响，提出了扩展的Thiele方程并探讨了曲率与自旋转矩之间的耦合关系。
AI.motivation: 磁性螺旋结构在固态物理和微纳技术中有广泛应用，研究曲率对其稳定性以及动力学行为具有重要意义。
AI.method: 推导了扩展的Thiele方程，纳入了张-李项以考虑曲面的影响，通过数值模拟分析了不同曲率条件下磁性螺旋的运动。
AI.result: 发现曲率与电流之间存在新的相互作用，磁性螺旋的运动轨迹受到曲率的显著影响，特别在高曲率区域出现了额外的Hall效应。
AI.conclusion: 本研究为曲率效应在磁性螺旋结构中的应用提供了新的理论框架，为微纳设备的设计与优化提供了理论支持。

title: Directly estimating the fidelity of measurement-based quantum computation
summary: In measurement-based quantum computation (MBQC), quantum circuits are implemented using adaptive measurements on an entangled resource state. In practice, the resource state will always be prepared with some noise, and it is crucial to understand the effect of this noise on the operation of MBQC. Typically, one measures the fidelity of the noisy resource state with the assumption that a high fidelity state means a high fidelity computation. However, the precise relationship between these two fidelities is not known. Here, we derive an expression that equates the average fidelity of the MBQC output state to a certain correlation function evaluated on the noisy resource state. Using this expression, we show that state fidelity provides a tight lower bound on average MBQC fidelity. Conversely, we also find that state fidelity can greatly underestimate average MBQC fidelity, implying that state fidelity is not a good indicator of MBQC performance in general. In response, we formulate an efficient method to directly estimate average MBQC fidelity by measuring the aforementioned correlation function. These results therefore improve our ability to characterize noisy resource states in quantum computers and benchmark MBQC performance.

AI.tldr: The paper explores how measurement noise impacts quantum computation, reveals its limitations in predicting computation accuracy, and proposes a new method to directly assess this impact.
AI.motivation: Improving quantum computer performance and reliability, especially in handling noisy resources.
AI.method: Mathematical derivation of fidelity relation, experimental setup for correlation function measurements.
AI.result: State fidelity is a less accurate measure for MBQC, new method directly correlates with computation fidelity.
AI.conclusion: Better understanding of noise effects improves MBQC characterization and benchmarking.

title: Noise and dynamics in acoustoelectric waveguides
summary: We present a quantum field theoretic formulation of acoustoelectric interactions in waveguide-like systems of arbitrary cross-section. Building on an open quantum systems approach, we derive a unified description of plasmon-phonon coupling that incorporates dissipation, noise, and the influence of drift currents. Our analysis captures both bulk and surface plasmon modes, highlighting how drift currents Doppler-shift plasmonic resonances and reshape the phonon noise spectrum. The resulting Heisenberg-Langevin equations yield closed-form expressions for frequency shifts, gain, and noise power spectra, enabling direct evaluation of performance metrics such as the noise factor in acoustoelectric amplifiers and oscillators. In the appropriate limits, this framework reproduces known results while extending them to complex geometries.

AI.tldr: Error
AI.motivation: Error
AI.method: Error
AI.result: Error
AI.conclusion: Error

title: Plasma impact on black hole shadow and gravitational weak lensing for Schwarzschild-like black hole
summary: This article delves into the observational properties of a Schwarzschild-like black hole (BH). Initially, the research provides a succinct examination of the spacetime geometry and the configuration of its horizon. Furthermore, we study the photon dynamics around the Schwarzschild-like BH in the presence of the plasma using the Hamiltonian formalism. It was found that the photon sphere radii increase under the influence of the plasma frequency and vice versa for the spacetime parameters. Further exploration is dedicated to understanding how the plasma affects the shadow of the BH, and we find that the radius of the BH shadow shrinks with the rise of the $ξ$ parameter and plasma frequency. We then turn to the getting constraint of the spacetime parameters and the plasma frequency by using the observational data released by the Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) collaboration for the M87* and Sgr A*. Additionally, the research scrutinises the phenomenon of gravitational weak lensing in the vicinity of a Schwarzschild-like BH, considering both uniform and non-uniform plasma scenarios. The outcomes demonstrate that the angle of deflection increases under the influence of a uniform plasma frequency, whereas the opposite is true for non-uniform plasma. In both scenarios, a rise in the spacetime parameters results in a decrease in the deflection angle. Finally, we investigate the magnification of the gravitationally lensed image. The effect of the spacetime parameters and plasma frequencies on the total magnification are same as in the deflection angles.

AI.tldr: 研究了萨 culd 像黑洞的观测性质，发现光动力学、阴影大小和透镜效应都受到等离子体影响。
AI.motivation: 探索等离子体环境对黑洞观测性质的影响，尤其是在萨 culd 像黑洞中。
AI.method: 使用哈密顿形式研究了光动力学，分析了阴影大小和透镜效应。
AI.result: 等离子体频率增加导致光球半径减小，阴影半径减小。透镜效应和放大效应随等离子体参数增加而增强。
AI.conclusion: 等离子体环境显著影响了萨 culd 像黑洞的观测性质，尤其是在光动力学、阴影和透镜效应方面。提供了解释实验结果的理论框架。

title: Flat-Band Generation in InAs/GaSb Quantum Wells through Vertically Engineered Heterostructures
summary: Quantum materials constitute a novel category of substances wherein quantum effects and electron-electron (e-e) interactions give rise to unforeseen phenomena on a macroscopic scale. Of particular interest within the realm of quantum materials are flat bands, which promote heavy conduction electrons and enhance e-e correlation effects. While the engineering of such flat bands has been demonstrated in graphene and two-dimensional transition metal dichalcogenides moiré superlattices and in lithography defined semiconductor moiré superlattices, conventional tear-and-stack fabrication methods face challenges due to inevitable twist-angle disorder, strain, and relaxation effects, leading to issues with reproducibility and scalability. Here, we explore the creation and modification of flat bands through vertically engineered III-V semiconductor heterostructures, without the need for twisting. These artificial quantum materials offer a reproducible and scalable means for producing high-quality flat-band materials via molecular beam epitaxy growth. Our investigation includes magnetotransport and infrared magneto-spectroscopy studies of quad-layer InAs/GaSb quantum wells, accompanied by k*p band structure calculations, which illustrate the flattening of bands in vertically designed heterostructures.

AI.tldr: 该论文利用纵结构的III-V半导体 heterostructures 技术成功设计出高质量的高能带材料，克服了传统方法的重组、应变等问题，具有可扩展性。
AI.motivation: 研究高能带材料对于理解新的宏观量子效应具有重要意义, 同时.rstrip.rstrip 传统方法的重组问题是一个重要的进步。
AI.method: 使用分子束外 epitaxial生长技术, 通过视觉设计的垂直结构实现高质量的高能带材料制造。
AI.result: 成功通过四层InAs/GaSb量子阱在磁转运和红外磁光光谱学中观察到高能带。
AI.conclusion: 该方法提供了一种可扩展的、高度可重复的高能带材料制造途径。

title: A Highly Sensitive Diamond NV Magnetometer Using Ramsey Interferometry with a Short Sensor-to-Sample Distance
summary: In this study, we developed a diamond quantum magnetometer based on Ramsey interferometry with a short sensor-to-sample distance. Conventional biomagnetic sensors with ensemble nitrogen-vacancy centers using continuous-wave optically detected magnetic resonance and Ramsey methods typically rely on watt-level lasers to achieve high sensitivity, resulting in thermal issues. In contrast, by employing the light-trapping diamond waveguide technique in a high-pressure and high-temperature diamond sample treated with electron beam irradiation, we obtained a high photon conversion efficiency of 9.5%, enabling us to simultaneously achieve a high sensitivity of 2.93(7) pT/Hz^1/2 in the 100-400 Hz frequency range and a minimal temperature increase of only approximately 13 K at a low laser power of 210 mW. Using a dry phantom designed to mimic magnetoencephalography signals, we measured a weak magnetic field of 77.7(2) pT without signal averaging at a sensor-to-sample distance of 2.5 mm. This short-distance measurement prevents severe spatial signal attenuation, yielding a high signal-to-noise ratio. The development here is crucial for practical biomagnetic applications based on Ramsey interferometry.

AI.tldr: 在本研究中，我们开发了一种基于Ramsey干涉仪的金刚石量子磁敏器，采用了短距离传感器样品之间的方法。传统自旋态检测传感器基于连续波光密检测磁性共振和Ramsay方法，通常依赖于瓦特级数的激光器以获得高灵敏度，但会导致热问题。通过采用光捕获金刚石波导技术在经过电子束辐照的高温高压金刚石样品中，我们获得了高达9.5%的光转换效率，在100-400赫兹频率范围内实现了高灵敏度2.93±7 pT/Hz^1/2，并且在低激光功率210毫瓦下仅增加了约13开尔文的温度。我们使用干燥的模拟-info枚头阵列测量了一个微弱磁场为77.7±2 pT，在2.5毫米传感器距离下的测量，避免了信号衰减，提供了较高的信噪比。这项研究对于基于Ramsey干涉仪的实用生物磁应用具有重要意义。
AI.motivation: 随着生物磁监测技术的发展，如何提高传感器的灵敏度和减少温度升高是一个关键问题。本研究通过优化传感器设计和材料技术，提出了一种高效且稳定的金刚石磁敏器，为实际应用提供了可行的解决方案。
AI.method: 本研究采用了光捕获金刚石波导技术，结合了Ramsey干涉仪的原理，并在经过辐照处理的高压高温金刚石样品中实现了高光转换效率和良好的磁敏性能。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该磁敏器在100-400赫兹频率范围内具有高灵敏度2.93±7 pT/Hz^1/2，并且在低激光功率下实现了仅有约13开尔文温升的性能。
AI.conclusion: 本研究成功开发了一种高性能的金刚石量子磁敏器，显著提高了灵敏度并减少了温升，为生物磁监测的实际应用奠定了基础。

title: A systematic design approach for one-dimensional and crossed photonic nanobeam cavities for quantum dot integration
summary: We present a systematic workflow for the design of one-dimensional photonic crystal nanobeam cavities with non-zero cavity lengths. By simultaneously optimizing the lattice periodicity, air-hole geometry, and cavity length, our approach enables precise control of optical confinement while mitigating radiative losses and linewidth broadening effects. The method is further extended to the design of crossed nanobeam cavities with both matching and mismatched resonance frequencies. This strategy significantly reduces the need for extensive parameter sweeps, providing an efficient route toward optimized cavity designs for integrated quantum photonic applications. Moreover, the resulting structures are inherently compatible with the integration of single-photon emitters.

AI.tldr: 设计了一维光子晶体纳米条纹腔，通过优化周期、空洞几何和长度，实现了光子被禁锢的同时减小了发射损失和 linewidth 的展宽。此外，还设计了交叉型同余和异相型纳米条纹腔，通过减少参数扫描，提升了量子光子集成应用的效率，且与单光子发射器兼容。
AI.motivation: 光子被被禁锢的纳米结构在量子光子ics中有广泛的应用，减少损失和提高效率是关键。
AI.method: 通过同时优化周期、空洞几何和长度，实现了光子被禁锢的同时减小了发射损失和 linewidth 的展宽。利用参数扫描和网络搜索方法计算.
AI.result: 优化后的结构成功地减小了参数扫描，提升了效率，且与单光子发射器兼容。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为光子ics中的优化设计提供了有效途径。

title: Mixed-State Entanglement in a Minimal Model of Quantum Chaos
summary: Understanding the dynamics of quantum correlations in many-body systems is a central problem in non-equilibrium quantum physics. We study the spread of mixed-state entanglement in a minimal model of quantum chaos, the kicked field Ising model. By combining the replica trick with the space-time duality of the model, we determine the exact spectrum of the partially transposed reduced density matrix. The resulting flat spectrum leads to exact relations between entanglement negativity, odd entropy and Rényi mutual information at early times. Numerical results further demonstrate that for equal tri-partitions and at late times, all entanglement measures saturate to the Haar-random values. In contrast, for unequal tri-partitions Rényi mutual information and negativity vanish at late times, implying that the corresponding reduced density matrix is factorizable. Extensive numerical simulations also show that the relation remains quantitatively valid for generic initial states, leading us to conjecture it for all initial states and all times.

AI.tldr: 文章探索了量子纠缠在许多体系统中的动力学行为，通过结合复制品技巧和时空对偶性，在kickeded场伊辛模型中精确计算了部分转置简并密度矩阵的谱。结果显示，在早期时刻，纠缠负性、奇熵和Rényi互相对信息之间存在精确的关系。数值结果表明，对于相等的三部分分割，在早期时刻，所有纠缠度量都会趋于Haar随机近似值。与非平衡量子物理中中心的挑战问题相关。对于不均衡的三部分分割，Rényi互相对信息和负性在网络后期趋于零，意味着相应的简并密度矩阵可以分解。通过大量数值模拟，作者发现该关系对于初始状态也相当定量地成立，从而推测对于所有初态和所有时间该关系均成立。
AI.motivation: 非平衡量子物理中 Understanding many-body system的量子关联动力学是一个中心问题。这项研究旨在通过kicked filed Ising模型，使用复制品技巧和时空对偶性，精确计算部分转置简并密度矩阵的谱，从而揭示纠缠连带性在早期和网络后期的行为。
AI.method: 在kickeded场伊辛模型中，结合复制品技巧和时空对偶性，精确计算部分转置简并密度矩阵的谱。通过数值模拟验证了所有纠缠度量在相等三部分分割和网络后期的行为，进一步推测其对所有初态和所有时间均有效。
AI.result: 通过计算部分转置简并密度矩阵的谱，精确确定了在早期时刻，纠缠负性、奇熵和Rényi互相对信息之间存在精确的关系。数值模拟表明，对于相等的三部分分割，所有纠缠度量在后期趋于Haar随机近似值。对于不均衡的三部分分割，Rényi互相对信息和负性在后期趋于零。通过大量数值模拟，作者发现该关系对于初始状态也相当定量地成立，从而推测该关系对所有初态和所有时刻均成立。
AI.conclusion: 这项研究在kickeded场伊辛模型中首次通过精确计算部分转置简并密度矩阵的谱，深入探讨了量子关联在许多体系统中的动力学行为。结果表明，在早期时刻，纠缠度量之间存在明确的关系，而在后期时刻，这些度量趋于Haar随机分布或完全分解。这些发现为理解非平衡量子物理中许多体系统的量子关联动力学提供了新的见解，并为实验研究提供了理论支持。

title: Approximate Models for Gravitational Memory
summary: The large-distance development of a sandwich gravitational wave, consistent with Carroll symmetry, provides us with a surprisingly good analytic approximation of the motion of particles in a wave with Pöschl-Teller profile. The role of the 2nd solution of the Stern-Liouville equation is highlighted. Similar results hold for Gaussian profiles.

AI.tldr: 这个摘要讨论了通过Carroll对称性构造的长距离引力波 sandwich模型，使用Pöschl-Teller势点近似了引力波中的粒子运动，并利用Stern-Liouville方程的第二解进行了分析。类似的结果也适用于高斯势函数。
AI.motivation: 引力波的研究在现代物理中非常重要，尤其是在引力波天文学的发展中，精准地理解引力波中的粒子运动对于后续的研究具有重要意义。
AI.method: 该摘要采用了Carroll对称性作为基础，结合了Pöschl-Teller势点和Stern-Liouville方程的第二解来分析引力波中的粒子运动。
AI.result: 研究结果表明，这种 sandwich 模型在长距离范围内具有良好的解析近似，并且结果适用于Pöschl-TellerPot和高斯势函数。
AI.conclusion: 通过这种 Sandwich 模型，可以更准确地描述引力波中的粒子运动，为后续引力波天文学的研究提供了有用的方法。

title: Probing the Penetration Depth of Topological Surface States by Magnetic Impurity Scattering in V-doped Sb$_2$Te$_3$
summary: Topological insulators host Dirac surface states (SS) protected by time-reversal symmetry. Inter-surface hybridization can gap the SS and give rise to the quantum spin Hall effect in films that are sufficiently thin compared to the SS penetration depth. However, quantifying the SS penetration depth typically requires painstaking synthesis of multiple films with varying thickness. Here we introduce a direct method to probe the SS penetration depth in bulk crystals, by studying the interplay between SS and magnetic impurities in \SVT. Using scanning tunneling microscopy and spectroscopy, we find that even sparse magnetic impurities ($\lesssim0.25\%$ vanadium) can gap the Dirac SS. However, a single V impurity induces only localized states, and does not form an impurity band, so the gapped Dirac dispersion is preserved away from the impurity. In high magnetic fields, we observe an energy shift of the $0^\text{th}$ Landau level and a suppression of quasiparticle lifetime at the Dirac point, indicating \newtext{magnetic} scattering of the SS. Crucially, by employing V impurities at different depths as precise scattering probes, we reveal the SS penetration depth on the sub-nanometer scale in a bulk crystal.

AI.tldr: 研究发现，范adium杂质（最多0.25%）在碲化物中可以成对靠近并excite Dirac表面态，从而导致能隙。通过范adium杂质的定位散射探针，他们测量出碲化物的Dirac表面态穿透深度在亚纳米级。
AI.motivation: 为了确切量化Dirac表面态的穿透深度，特别是在碲化物这种无机晶体中，需要一种直接的方法，而不需要制备不同厚度的多片。
AI.method: 使用扫描隧道显微镜和光谱学，研究了碲化物中稀有范adium杂质的作用，发现这些杂质可以在不同深度定位散射，从而测定了Dirac表面态的穿透深度。
AI.result: 首次在 bulk 物质中用单一杂质精确测量 Dirac 表面态的穿透深度，结果在亚纳米级。
AI.conclusion: Dirac表面态在碲化物中的穿透深度可以精确测量，且稀有范adium杂质是有效的探针，这为该领域提供了新方法。

title: Higher order Magnus expansions for two-level quantum dynamics
summary: We investigate the Magnus expansion for a generic time-dependent two-level system under single-axis driving. By virtue of the \(\mathfrak{su}(2)\) Lie algebra, the expansion is decomposed into a commutator-free form. To illustrate the usefulness of the gained expression, we then revisit the Landau-Zener-Stückelberg-Majorana model, with a focus on non-adiabatic transitions as well as the Stokes phase. In addition, the semiclassical Rabi model is systematically treated by determining the Floquet quasienergy up to different orders. We demonstrate how to employ suitable picture transformations as well as on how to enforce the symmetry of the underlying model in order to guarantee convergence of the expansion as well as to achieve satisfactory agreement with the exact results. For both models that we studied it turns out that a third order approximation yields results that are in next to perfect agreement with exact analytical ones. Surprisingly, in the case of the semiclassical Rabi model, even the second order Magnus approximation in the adiabatic picture produces almost exact results over the whole parameter range.

AI.tldr: The paper examines the Magnus expansion for a two-level system driven by a single axis, revealing that a third-order approximation closely matches exact results. It also shows that the second-order Magnus approximation in the adiabatic picture is highly accurate for the semiclassical Rabi model across parameter ranges.
AI.motivation: The motivation stems from the need to understand and simplify the Magnus expansion for two-level systems, particularly in the context of non-adiabatic transitions and Stokes phases.
AI.method: The authors employ Lie algebra (	extit{	extg}-algebra) techniques and Floquet theory to derive the Magnus expansion, focusing on commutator-free forms and their applications.
AI.result: The study demonstrates that the Magnus expansion yields highly accurate results when truncated at the third order for the Landau-Zener-Stückelberg-Majorana model and even better results at the second order for the semiclassical Rabi model.
AI.conclusion: This research provides valuable insights into using the Magnus expansion for diverse two-level systems, highlighting the effectiveness of different truncation orders based on the system under study.

title: Direct Waves in Black-Hole Binary Mergers: Insights from the Backwards One Body Model
summary: The merger-ringdown radiation from a black hole binary merger is accurately modeled by a sum of linear quasinormal modes (QNMs). Recently, a non-QNM ``direct wave" component of the radiation, associated with prompt emission from a plunging perturber, has been identified. Motivated by the behavior of null geodesics perturbed from the remnant light ring, the Backwards One Body (BOB) approach has been shown to model the full merger-ringdown radiation to high accuracy, while using only a minimal number of parameters. In this work, using the Pöschl--Teller potential, we first show how the BOB amplitude evolution can be recovered from the QNM pole contributions. We then apply rational filters to isolate the non-QNM content in BOB and numerical relativity waveforms. We show that BOB naturally captures the direct wave component of the merger radiation, explaining its accuracy near the waveform peak. Finally, we use BOB to show that the direct wave frequency is largely uncorrelated with the horizon frequency, even for high spin remnants, and instead tracks the News frequency at the time of the peak News amplitude.

AI.tldr: BOB 模型能准确描述黑洞双星合并和回环辐射, BOBParametersModifications能捕获非站定振荡模式。
AI.motivation: 研究黑洞双星合并和事件视界 telescope望远镜附近区域的辐射特性, 特别是对 prompt emission和回环辐射的解释。
AI.method: 用 Backwards One Body (BOB) 方法建模合并辐射, BOBParametersModifications识别非驻定振荡波段; 利用 Pöschl--Teller势恢复 BOB 振幅演化, 并用有理滤波器分离 BOB 和数值相对论波形中的非驻定振荡内容。
AI.result: BOB 模型能高精度捕捉回环辐射中的直接波段, BOB频率在事件视界随着轨道角动量增加而增加, 但直接波频率与黑洞 Event Horizon 的频率在高自旋情况下显著不同, 更接近峰值时刻的 news 波动频率。
AI.conclusion: 研究证实了 BOB 模型在描述黑洞双星合并和回环辐射中的直接波段的准确性, 并展示了直接波频率与黑洞视界频率的关系。

title: Quantum electrometry in a silicon carbide power device
summary: For high-bias operation devices such as silicon carbide (SiC) power devices, early detection of failure mechanisms is essential to ensure reliability. This requires a method to map high electric fields with high spatial resolution, which has not been realized until now. Here we report that the silicon vacancy (Vsi) in SiC has outstanding characteristics for detecting electric fields applied in various directions within a high-biased SiC device. Vsi exhibits an equivalent response to electric field components parallel (Epara) and perpendicular (Eperp) to the c-axis, a feature unique among quantum sensors, and the responsiveness to Epara and Eperp enables detection of arbitrary electric fields encountered in cutting-edge SiC power devices. We confirmed high electric field detection of ~2.3 MV/cm, which is ~90% of the breakdown electric field of a 4H-SiC with typical carrier concentration. Selectively formed Vsi enables high-resolution mapping of electric field distribution. Vsi-based quantum sensors bring data-driven research and development methodologies as well as device degradation diagnosis.

AI.tldr: 该研究提出了一种基于硅碳onyte（Vsi）的新方法来实现高空间分辨率的高电场检测，这对SiC功率器件的可靠性至关重要。
AI.motivation: 随着硅碳onyte在高有 bias运算器件中的广泛应用，如电力转换和高频开关，对检测和管理这些器件中的高电场具有重要意义。高电场可能导致器件失效，因此需要一种高效、高分辨率的方法来监测和分析电场分布。
AI.method: 研究利用硅碳onyte中的Vsi位置敏感特征，能够感知平行和垂直于晶体轴的电场分量，这种方法能够检测高达2.3 MV/cm的电场，接近于4H-silicon carbide的-breakdown电场。此外，通过选择性刻蚀方法，实现了对电场分布的高分辨率成像。
AI.result: 研究结果表明，利用Vsi检测器可以准确检测高达2.3 MV/cm的电场，并且可以在实际应用中接近silicon carbide的-breakdown电场。此外，通过选择性刻蚀形成Vsi，还实现了对电场分布的高分辨率成像。这种方法不仅能够实时捕捉电场变化，还为硅碳onyte在高性能电力电子器件中的应用提供了理论支持和实验验证。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为硅碳onyte功率器件的可靠性提供了创新的解决方案。通过位置敏感的Vsi检测器及其高分辨率成像技术，可以有效监控高电场分布，为这些器件的优化设计和故障诊断提供了可行的方法。未来的研究可能进一步优化检测器的性能，并将其应用于更复杂的电力电子设备中。

title: Millimeter Wave Readout of a Superconducting Qubit
comment: 5 pages, 4 figures in main text. 9 pages, 10 figures, 1 table in supplemental materials
summary: Millimeter waves are emerging as an enabling technology for connecting and enhancing different quantum platforms such as Rydberg atoms, optomechanics, and superconducting qubits. In this work, we focus on the interaction between millimeter wave photons and conventional transmon qubits, specifically for qubit readout. We study a circuit quantum electrodynamic (cQED) system consisting of a millimeter-wave cavity at $ω_r = 2π\times 34.7$ GHz and a transmon qubit at $ω_q = 2π\times 3.1$ GHz coupled at rate $g = 2π\times 1.3$ GHz. With such a large detuning between cavity and qubit, $ω_r/ω_q > 10$, we are able to suppress drive induced unwanted state transitions, enabling strong drives for qubit readout. We measure no resonant state transitions up to $1,000$ drive photons and readout the qubit state with more than $100$ photons to achieve a measurement fidelity greater than 99% without the aid of a quantum limited amplifier.

AI.tldr: 该论文通过在毫米波腔和超导体电容电感器之间引入大 detuning，成功实现了对超导体电容电感器的高保真度读出。
AI.motivation: 毫米波技术在量子平台之间的通信和增强具有重要应用价值，该论文通过引入大 detuning 实现了精确的 qubit 读出。
AI.method: 通过使用电路量子电动力学系统，研究了毫米波腔和超导体电容电感器之间的相互作用，选择了适当的参数以获得高保真度的 qubit 读出。
AI.result: 通过这种方法，能够在没有量子受限放大器的情况下，实现 qubit 读出的高保真度，并且在 1,000 激光光子后仍能保持良好的状态转移抑制能力。
AI.conclusion: 该研究展示了通过调整毫米波腔和超导体电容电感器的频率，可以有效改善 qubit 读出的性能，为量子计算和通信提供了新的技术路线。

title: Evidence of Uncollapsed Quantum Amplitudes After Consecutive Measurements
summary: Two of the most common interpretations of quantum measurement disagree about the fate of quantum amplitudes after measurement, yet this disagreement has not previously led to experimentally distinguishable predictions. In the standard collapse picture, commonly linked to the Copenhagen interpretation of quantum mechanics, measurements eliminate unrealized amplitudes without leaving a memory. In contrast, in the unitary theory, the measurement device registers one of the possible outcomes while remaining part of an entangled state that continues to harbor the unrealized amplitudes. This persistence arises naturally under unitary evolution, since a measurement device that is part of an entangled system cannot serve as a faithful probe of the joint quantum state. Using single-photon measurements of a tunable quantum state, we experimentally show that these two theories make different predictions when three or more consecutive measurements are performed on the same quantum system. Analysis of the joint density matrix of the three measurements reveals coherence among them and supports the unitary theory of quantum measurement. When decoherence is explicitly introduced, the joint density matrix of the quantum system of interest and the apparatus becomes consistent with what a collapse theory would predict. This work clarifies the dynamics of consecutive quantum measurements and offers new insights into the interpretation of quantum measurements.

AI.tldr: The paper discusses the disagreement between the standard collapse and unitary interpretations of quantum measurement, particularly when multiple consecutive measurements are performed. It demonstrates through experiments that these interpretations make different predictions and supports the unitary theory by analyzing the joint density matrix using single-photon measurements and decoherence analysis.
AI.motivation: Understanding the behavior of quantum measurements and their interpretations is fundamental to quantum mechanics. This paper aims to resolve discrepancies in the predictions of two interpretations by performing experimental tests with consecutive measurements.
AI.method: The research involved performing single-photon measurements on a tunable quantum state and analyzing the joint density matrix of three consecutive measurements. They compared the results under different decoherence conditions to test the predictions of the standard collapse and unitary theories.
AI.result: The study found that when three consecutive measurements are made on the same quantum system, the standard collapse theory and the unitary theory make different predictions. The experimental results aligned more closely with the unitary theory, which accounts for the persistence of unrealized amplitudes without a separate measuring apparatus memory. When decoherence was introduced, the results were consistent with the collapse theory predictions.
AI.conclusion: The paper contributes to the understanding of quantum measurement dynamics, particularly in scenarios involving multiple consecutive measurements. It provides empirical evidence supporting the unitary theory in specific contexts and highlights the challenges in distinguishing the two interpretations experimentally when multiple measurements are involved.

title: Readout-induced degradation of transmon lifetimes: interplay of TLSs and qubit spectral reshaping
summary: Measurement backaction degrades dispersive readout of superconducting qubits even at modest drive strengths, often via the reduction of qubit lifetimes during readout. In this work, we theoretically and experimentally study this degradation and show how it can result from the interplay between detuned two-level systems (TLSs) and a drive-renormalized qubit spectrum. For modest to strong readout, the qubit emission spectrum becomes non-Lorentzian and depends sensitively on the readout drive frequency (even when measurement rate is fixed). We combine the readout-modified qubit emission spectrum with time-dependent perturbation theory to predict qubit lifetimes in the presence of a TLS bath. Master equation simulations and experimental measurements on a frequency-tunable transmon confirm these predictions quantitatively. In particular, we find that driving at the resonator frequency associated with the qubit ground state yields the narrowest qubit emission spectrum and the least lifetime degradation for a fixed measurement rate, providing a practical guideline for optimizing readout protocols in future quantum processors.

AI.tldr: Measurement backaction degrades dispersive readout of superconducting qubits even at modest drive strengths, often via the reduction of qubit lifetimes during readout.
AI.motivation: This work addresses the challenge of measurement backaction in superconducting qubits, which is crucial for optimizing readout protocols in quantum processors.
AI.method: The study combines theoretical analysis using time-dependent perturbation theory and master equation simulations with experimental measurements on a frequency-tunable transmon qubit.
AI.result: The researchers find that driving at the resonator frequency associated with the qubit ground state yields the narrowest qubit emission spectrum and the least degradation of qubit lifetimes for a fixed measurement rate.
AI.conclusion: This research provides a quantitative understanding of the interplay between qubit emission, readout drive, and measurement backaction, offering practical guidelines for optimizing readout protocols in future quantum processors.

title: Engineering walk-off-induced orbital angular momentum spectrum in spontaneous parametric downconversion
summary: Spontaneous parametric downconversion (SPDC) has been considered as a reliable source of high- dimensional entangled states in orbital angular momentum (OAM) basis. In real-world experiments, the spatial walk-off of the pump often degrades the fidelity of the generated quantum state. Since the walk-off effect breaks the rotational symmetry of the system, the conservation of total OAM is violated. Although the compensation of walk-off effects has become a well-established experimental technique, a systematic modal analysis of the spatial walk-off effect is still incomplete for SPDC. Here, we quantitatively analyze the violation of OAM conservation due to the pump walk-off effect in SPDC processes. We have derived a scaling law of the total OAM distribution with respect to the pump walk-off angle. We have also explored the feasibility of using the spatial walk-off as a mechanism to engineer the generated quantum state. Our study has provided guidelines for the generation of OAM-entangled state under realistic experimental conditions.

title: Quantum-classical diagnostics and Bohmian inequivalence for higher time-derivative Hamiltonians
summary: We develop a Bohmian analysis of a two-dimensional ghost Hamiltonian and its mapping to the degenerate Pais-Uhlenbeck model. Using Gaussian wavepackets, we derive the corresponding guidance equations, the centre and width evolution, and the quantum potential. We use these quantities to characterise bounded, quasi-semiclassical, spiral, and runaway regimes. The Bohmian trajectories provide a direct dynamical diagnostic of coherence, packet deformation, and quantum-classical separation. We then compare a bi-Hamiltonian pair consisting of the ghost Hamiltonian and a classically equivalent alternative formulation. While the two descriptions produce identical classical trajectories, they lead to different Bohmian trajectories and different quantum potentials evaluated along those trajectories. This demonstrates that classical equivalence need not extend to Bohmian quantum dynamics and identifies a concrete quantum ambiguity in the degenerate higher-derivative system.

AI.tldr: 研究展示了 ghost 哈密顿与Pais-Uhlenbeck模型的Bohmian动力学性质及其量子不确定性。
AI.motivation: 探索 ghost 哈密顿在Bohmian视角下的动力学行为和量子效应。
AI.method: 使用二维Bohmian理论，分析了高阶导数系统的波包演化和量子势。
AI.result: 发现在不同Hamiltonian下Bohmian轨迹和量子势不同，表明经典等效性不延伸到Bohmian动力学。
AI.conclusion: 高阶导数系统的Bohmian行为表现出量子不明确性，经典对量子动力学的影响存在限制。

title: Benchmarking quantum simulation with neutron-scattering experiments
summary: A central goal of quantum computation is the realistic simulation of quantum materials. Although quantum processors have advanced rapidly in scale and fidelity, it has remained unclear whether pre-fault-tolerant devices can perform quantitatively reliable material simulations within their limited gate budgets. Here, we demonstrate that a superconducting quantum processor operating on up to 50 qubits can already produce meaningful, quantitative comparisons with inelastic neutron-scattering measurements of KCuF$_3$, a canonical realization of a gapless Luttinger liquid system with a strongly correlated ground state and a spectrum of emergent spinons. The quantum simulation is enabled by a quantum-classical workflow for computing dynamical structure factors (DSFs). The resulting spectra are benchmarked against experimental measurements using multiple metrics, highlighting the impact of circuit depth and circuit fidelity on simulation accuracy. Finally, we extend our simulations to 1D XXZ Heisenberg model with next-nearest neighbor interactions and a strong anisotropy, producing a gapped excitation spectrum, which could be used to describe the CsCoX$_3$ compounds above the Néel temperature. Our results establish a framework for computing DSFs for quantum materials in classically challenging regimes of strong entanglement and long-range interactions, enabling quantum simulations that are directly testable against laboratory measurements.

AI.tldr: 成功利用50量子比特超导处理器生成与实验数据精度匹配的定量对比，并为量子材料的计算机仿真奠定了框架。
AI.motivation: 探索在小规模量子处理器下进行可靠量子材料仿真的方法；对量子计算在材料科学中的潜在应用进行深入研究。
AI.method: 开发了量子经典结合 workflow来计算动力学结构因子(DSFs)，并使用多种指标比较了实验测值和仿真结果。
AI.result: 实现了对KCuF3Canonical Luttinger液体系及1D XXZ Heisenberg模型的量子仿真，展示了在小规模量子处理器下进行复杂量子材料研究的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 为小规模量子处理器在量子材料仿真中的应用提供了理论依据和实际案例，展示了量子计算在解决复杂量子体系问题中的潜力。

title: Kirkwood-Dirac classical states based on discrete Fourier transform: Representation with directed graph
summary: The Kirkwood-Dirac (KD) quasiprobability distribution is a fundamental representation for quantum states and has been widely applied in quantum metrology, quantum chaos, weak values in recent years. A quantum state is KD-classical if its KD-quasiprobability distribution forms a valid classical probability distribution with respect to two given bases, and KD-nonclassical otherwise, with the latter being closely associated with quantum advantages in various quantum processes. In this work, we investigate the structural characteristics of the KD-classical state set when the transition matrix between two orthonormal bases takes the form of a discrete Fourier transform (DFT) matrix. First, we adopt an alternative analytical approach to prove that the set of KD-classical states in a $p^r$-dimensional Hilbert space is the convex hull of KD-classical pure states--a conclusion that was recently established by De Bi{è}vre et al [Annales Henri Poincar{é}, 1-20, 2025]. Furthermore, we define a directed graph and use it to characterize KD-classical pure states in a Hilbert space of arbitrary dimension $d$. That is, the convex hull of KD-classical pure states along any path from the start vertex to the end vertex in this directed graph is exactly the intersection of the KD-classical state set and the linear space spanned by these path-associated KD-classical pure states. This general result not only yields the $p^r$-dimensional conclusion in a straightforward manner but also encompasses Theorem 2 in the existing work [J. Phys. A, 57, 435303, 2024], demonstrating its generality and inclusiveness.

AI.tldr: 这项研究探讨了在离散傅里叶变换矩阵转换下KD-经典态集合的结构特征。作者通过构造有向图，提供了KD-经典纯态的表征方法。同时，他们证明了KD-经典态集合在p^r维空间内可以表示为KD-经典纯态的凸包。该结论简洁明了，有效地解释了KD-经典态与量子优势的关系。
AI.motivation: 量子态的KD-经典性在量子 metrology、量子 chaos等前沿领域具有重要意义。深入研究KD-经典态的结构特征不仅有助于理解量子态的性质，也为开发新的量子技术提供了理论依据。
AI.method: 该论文采用了基于有向图的分析方法，系统地描述了KD-经典纯态的结构，并通过凸包理论明确KD-经典态集合的组成。
AI.result: 研究表明，离散傅里叶变换矩阵转换下的KD-经典态集合在p^r维空间内可以表示为KD-经典纯态的凸包。该结果不仅简化了KD-经典态的研究，还拓展了现有相关理论的适用范围。
AI.conclusion: 这项研究通过创新的方法，成功揭示了KD-经典态的结构特性，为量子态分类和量子技术发展提供了重要理论支持。

title: Investigating Lipkin-Meshkov-Glick Model and Criticality-Enhanced Metrology in a Coherent Ising Machine
summary: Quantum criticality has received extensive attention due to its ability to significantly enhance quantum sensing. But its realization and control in many-body quantum systems remain challenging. We present an effective scheme to simulate the Lipkin-Meshkov-Glick (LMG) model using a coherent Ising machine (CIM) composed of a network of degenerate optical parametric oscillators (DOPO). In our work, the spin variables of the LMG model are mapped onto the phases of DOPO pulses, and the spin-spin interactions are realized by all-to-all couplings among them. Through our investigation of the critical behavior in the antiferromagnetically coupled LMG model in the thermodynamic limit, i.e., $N\rightarrow\infty$, and its application in quantum sensing near the critical point, we verify that the CIM does not only effectively capture the second-order quantum phase transition (QPT) at the critical point but also reconstructs its complete phase diagram under ferromagnetic coupling. Furthermore, we demonstrate how the critical dynamics of this simulation platform can be utilized for quantum-enhanced metrology, achieving a measurement precision that diverges near the critical point of the LMG model. These results highlight the capability of the CIM as a flexible experimental platform for investigating the QPT in the fundamental quantum magnetic models, providing valuable insights into quantum simulation and critical phenomena.

AI.tldr: This paper presents a coherent Ising machine (CIM) using degenerate optical parametric oscillators (DOPO) to simulate the LMG model, demonstrating effective control of quantum phase transitions and critical phenomena for quantum sensing and metrology.
AI.motivation: Quantum criticality in many-body systems is crucial for quantum sensing applications, but practical implementation is challenging. This paper addresses these challenges by developing a CIM to simulate the LMG model.
AI.method: The authors map spin variables of the LMG model onto the phases of DOPO pulses and implement all-to-all spin-spin interactions through coupling among them.
AI.result: The CIM successfully captures the second-order QPT and reconstructs the complete phase diagram under ferromagnetic coupling. It also enables quantum-enhanced metrology with diverging measurement precision near the critical point.
AI.conclusion: This work demonstrates the potential of CIM as a flexible platform for studying QPT in quantum magnetic models, offering new insights into quantum simulation and critical phenomena.

title: Thermodynamics of a biophotomimetic nonreciprocal quantum battery
summary: We propose a theoretical model of a fully functional nonreciprocal quantum battery inspired by the architecture of bacterial light-harvesting complexes. We assign functional roles to collective quantum optical subradiant and superradiant states and introduce a unimodal cavity to assist storage. The transition rates are obtained from an effective non-Hermitian Hamiltonian, tailored to the battery geometry which are fed into a master equation to unravel the time evolution. We investigate the complete thermodynamic performance including storage, leakage, ergotropy, work extraction, flux, and power. We observe optimization at different ring sizes, each peaking at its specific energetic function. Strong coupling between the ring and central system enhances the battery's ability to store energy but reduces the ability of power output. The ergotropy exceeds capacity and approaches it linearly with increasing system size, with an optimal small-size regime that disappears under strong coupling.

title: Generalized Inverses of Quantum Channels: a categorical perspective
summary:
A quantum channel is defined as being completely positive (CP) and trace preserving (TP). While not every quantum channel is invertible or reversible, every quantum channel admits various kinds of generalized inverses such as the Moore-Penrose inverse and the Drazin inverse. A generalized inverse of a quantum channel may not itself be a quantum channel: it often fails to be CP. However, generalized inverses still play an important role in quantum error mitigation. Here, because it is often desirable for the generalized inverse of a quantum channel to be at least TP, the Drazin inverse, which is TP, is favoured over the Moore-Penrose inverse, which is not in general TP.
  In this paper, we take a categorical perspective on generalized inverses of quantum channels. This allows us to give a simple proof of the fact that the Drazin inverse of a quantum channel is always TP. It also allows us to show that for unital quantum channels, the Drazin inverse is also unital. We then generalize this result to dagger Drazin inverses, which allows us to show that for unital quantum channels, the Moore-Penrose inverse is both TP and unital as well. This opens the door to new applications of both the Drazin inverse and Moore-Penrose inverse in quantum information theory and, in particular, in quantum error mitigation.

title: Simulating the Open System Dynamics of Multiple Exchange-Only Qubits using Subspace Monte Carlo
summary: We propose a Monte Carlo based method for simulating the open system dynamics of multiple exchange-only (EO) qubits. In the EO encoding, the total spin projection quantum number along the $z$-axis of the three constituent spins remains unchanged under exchange operations, in contrast to the open system (or multi-qubit miscalibration) setting where coherent and incoherent mixing of states with different quantum numbers occurs. In our approach, we choose to measure the total spin component along the $z$-axis of each EO qubit after every logical quantum operation, which decoheres coherent mixtures of states with different spin projection quantum numbers. Independent simulations thus give different trajectories of the system in the associated subspaces, so we refer to this method as the Subspace Monte Carlo method. With each EO qubit having a definite spin projection quantum number, the density matrix of $n$ qubits can be represented by a vector of dimension $3^{2n}$, instead of $8^{2n}$, with an additional vector of dimension $n$ to label the quantum number of each qubit. We show that this approximation of the dynamics remains faithful to the true dynamics when the simulated circuits twirl the noise, converting coherent errors to stochastic errors, which can be achieved using randomized compiling. We use this simulation approach to study how correlations in measurement outcomes of circuits with reset-if-leaked gadgets, such as a multi-round Bell state stabilization circuit that uses 6 EO qubits, are affected by the choice of CNOT implementations.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种基于蒙特卡罗方法模拟多个交换型量子比特（EO qubits）开放系统动力学的方法。在EO编码中，三个基团的总自旋投影量子数沿z轴保持不变，而与开放系统或 多重失准设定相反。在我们的方法中，在每次逻辑量子操作后测量每个EO qubit沿z轴的总自旋投影量子数，这种测量会将不同量子数状态下 的相干混合物 decohere。独立模拟会给出系统在相应子空间中的不同轨迹，因此我们将此方法称为子空间蒙特卡罗方法。每个EO qubit都有一个确定的自旋投影量子数，因此n个 qubit的密度矩阵可以用一个大小为3^{2n} 的向量表示，再加上一个大小为n的向量来标记每个 qubit 的量子数。我们表明，在模拟电路绕混噪声时，这种近似仍能忠实地反映真实动力学，无需任何额外的绕混处理即可介导相干错误为随机错误。我们使用这种模拟方法研究了带有重置如果泄漏机制的电路（例如使用6个EO qubits 的多轮Bell态稳定 化电路）的测量结果相关性，受CNOT实现方式的影响。
AI.motivation: 开发高效的方法模拟量子比特动力学对于量子计算的重要性和原创性来说是至关重要的。特别是在开放系统中，节点之间互相干扰严重，所以如何准确地模拟这些复杂系统的行为对研究者来说具有挑战性。
AI.method: 蒙特卡罗方法与子空间分解相结合。在每个时间步长后，独立地对每个EO qubit的沿z轴的总自旋投影量子数进行测量，将每个 Time Evolution 的状态限制在特定的子空间中，从而降低了整个系统的复杂度。特别是引入了辅助向量来标记每个 qubit 的量子数，从而最大限度地简化了计算和存储需求。
AI.result: 提出的方法有效缩小了模拟空间的维度，这在高阶量子系统中尤其重要。尤其是当量子系统规模扩大时，传统方法由于维度指数爆炸而导致无法进行有效计算。通过子空间蒙特卡罗方法，我们能够以线性增长的复杂度来处理这些复杂度高的问题。
AI.conclusion: 提供了一种新的策略，能够有效地模拟复杂的多量子比特开放系统，这对量子计算的理论研究和实际应用开发具有重要意义。

title: Collective Nuclear Polaritons with Coherent and Tunable Excitation Dynamics
summary: We propose collective nuclear polaritons formed by hybridizing a 229Th nuclear ensemble with a vacuum-ultraviolet cavity mode generated via four-wave mixing, achieving a collective light-matter coupling that scales as $\sqrt{N}$. In the strong-coupling regime the system displays vacuum Rabi oscillations, indicating the hybridization between cavity photons and nuclear excitations. In the superradiant regime, the stored excitation is released in a cooperative burst with peak intensity scaling as $N^2$. The emission lifetime shrinks from thousands of seconds to the millisecond scale and remains tunable. Detuning sweeps across the polariton avoided crossing allow adiabatic conversion of the photonic excitation into a collective nuclear excitation, enabling reversible quantum storage. Our results demonstrate that cavity-mediated nuclear polaritons enable deterministic lifetime engineering and coherent quantum storage in nuclear systems.

title: Folding-Free Zero-Noise Extrapolation by Layout-induced Noise Diversity
summary: Near term quantum processors operate in a noise dominated regime, motivating error mitigation techniques that recover accurate expectation values without full fault tolerance. Zero Noise Extrapolation (ZNE) is a widely used but biased error mitigation method that lacks rigorous error bounds. Its effective application requires nontrivial technical choices, most notably the selection of noise scaling factors and extrapolation models, making ZNE sensitive to user expertise and often necessitating costly trial and error procedures. Here, we introduce Folding Free Zero Noise Extrapolation (FF-ZNE), a method that removes the need for noise factor selection by achieving effective noise amplification without circuit folding. FF-ZNE exploits isomorphic hardware layouts with distinct native noise profiles, such that executing a fixed circuit across these layouts induces controllable variations in the effective noise strength. Under a depolarizing noise model, we analytically show that the resulting extrapolation admits a fixed linear form, eliminating extrapolator choice and enabling a seamless, user independent mitigation procedure. We further propose two algorithms that identify sets of isomorphic hardware layouts on which a given circuit yields sufficiently distinct expectation values to enable reliable zero noise extrapolation. Experiments on a 133 qubit IBM Quantum device demonstrate that FF-ZNE yields mitigated expectation values with average deviations of ~6% and 4.5% for up to 50 qubit EfficientSU2 (sparse) and Hamiltonian simulation (dense) circuits, respectively. The method is thus scalable and applicable to a broad range of circuits. By eliminating noise factor and extrapolator selection, FF-ZNE transforms zero noise extrapolation from a technique requiring expert tuning into a practical, scalable, and broadly accessible error mitigation method for current quantum hardware.

AI.tldr: FF-ZNE is a method for error mitigation in quantum computing that removes the need for selecting noise scaling factors and extrapolation models, making the process more user-friendly and effective for a broader range of circuits.
AI.motivation: Quantum processors operate in a noisy environment, and error mitigation techniques are essential to recover accurate results without full fault tolerance. ZNE is a widely used method, but it lacks rigorous error bounds and requires expert tuning, making it costly and impractical for many users. FF-ZNE addresses these issues by eliminating the need for noise scaling factor selection and provides a scalable solution for a wide range of quantum circuits.
AI.method: FF-ZNE achieves effective noise amplification without circuit folding by executing a fixed circuit across quantum processors with distinct native noise profiles. This allows for controllable variations in effective noise strength. Under a depolarizing noise model, the extrapolation process results in a fixed linear form, removing the need for extrapolator choice and simplifying the mitigation procedure. Two algorithms are proposed to identify sets of isomorphic hardware layouts that enable reliable zero noise extrapolation.
AI.result: Experiments on a 133-qubit IBM Quantum device show that FF-ZNE yields mitigated expectation values with average deviations of approximately 6% for 50-qubit EfficientSU2 (sparse) circuits and 4.5% for Hamiltonian simulation (dense) circuits. This demonstrates that FF-ZNE is scalable and effective across different circuit types.
AI.conclusion: By removing the need for expert tuning and providing a consistent and reliable method for noise extrapolation, FF-ZNE enhances the practicality and accessibility of error mitigation techniques for current quantum hardware.

title: Revisited Quantification of the Resource Theory of Imaginarity
summary: In this paper, we investigate the decay behaviors of three imaginarity-related metrics, specifically the $l_1$-norm-based imaginarity measure, imaginarity robustness, and imaginarity relative entropy, for arbitrary single-qubit pure initial states under three typical quantum channels: dephasing, generalized amplitude damping, and phase-amplitude damping. Furthermore, we extend our analysis to higher-dimensional systems by examining the decay trends of the aforementioned imaginarity metrics for several key two-qubit states under two-qubit channels. We also generalize the concept of the maximal imaginary state (originally defined for single qubits in the resource theory of imaginarity) to separable two-qubit states. In addition, we extend the definitions of imaginary power and de-imaginary power for single-qubit channels to two-qubit channels acting on separable two-qubit states. Finally, we compute the imaginary and de-imaginary powers for several common two-qubit channels.
